message:
fix(unittest): assertEqual now uses class __eq__ methods

Added assertEqualGeneric runtime helper that properly handles custom
class comparisons by:

1. Checking at comptime if either argument has __eq__ method
2. If yes, calling classInstanceEq which invokes __eq__
3. If no, falling back to toPyValue().eql() for primitive comparison

This fixes assertEqual(Rat(2,3) + Rat(1,3), 1) where Rat.__eq__ needs
to be called to compare the Rat instance with an int.

The previous approach of checking isClassInstance() at codegen time
was unreliable because type inference doesn't always correctly track
return types through method chains (e.g., Rat.__floordiv__ returns int
but inference might return unknown).
